summary: Frontend-only SEO/EEAT verification of Muk Buddy after GitHub blog sync. Tested homepage, /ads, /about, 7 blog posts, llms.txt, both lead forms (gtag + fbq tracking with dynamic value), JSON-LD Product schema, robots meta, and patent-pending copy consistency. Most things work; 3 HIGH severity bugs found.

ui_bugs.issue: robots meta is 'index, follow, max-image-preview:large' — spec requires 'noindex, follow'. /ads is being indexed when it should be intentionally noindex. Likely PageHead/Helmet for /ads is being overridden by the site-wide default; check /app/frontend/src/pages/AdsLanding.jsx PageHead/Helmet usage and prerender output (local build/ads/index.html DOES contain the correct 'noindex, follow' on a second meta tag — but the preview server serves the unprerendered SPA shell so only the site-wide 'index, follow' meta survives).

ui_bugs.component: /about — body copy contains forbidden phrase 'US patent application'
ui_bugs.issue: About page reads: 'Floor Lord Industries filed a US patent application on the 2-chamber design. It is currently patent pending in the United…'. The spec explicitly forbids 'US patent' on /about (says: 'patent pending (not patented or US patent)'). Rewrite to: 'Floor Lord Industries has filed for a 2-chamber design patent — it is currently patent pending in the United States.' or similar.

Author byline is 'By Muk Buddy' (not 'By Jason Brouk') AND the canonical 'Founder of Floor Lord Industries and inventor of Muk Buddy. 28 years doing floors — and still building the tools contractors wish already existed.' bio block is missing from this post. All 6 other blog posts have it. Likely the .md frontmatter for this post was overwritten during the GitHub sync and lost its author field. Fix the .md frontmatter and rerun /app/scripts/build-blog.js.
ui_bugs.selector: blog post author byline + author bio block

ui_bugs.issue: The preview server (Express, x-powered-by: Express on port 3000) returns the SAME 28KB public/index.html SPA shell for /, /about, /ads, and all /blog/* routes — instead of serving the prerendered HTML in /app/frontend/build/{route}/index.html (which is correctly built locally at 49KB+ each with all SEO content baked in). For example local build/about/index.html is 49,330 bytes with the h1, full body copy, and per-route meta — but curl /about returns the 28,076-byte shell. This kills crawler-visible SEO (h1, JSON-LD, meta robots, title, etc.). The runtime React app still hydrates correctly so human users see everything, but bots see almost nothing. Confirm the server config (likely scripts/serve.js or similar) actually serves the prerendered build directory routes.

verified_passing: Homepage h1 contains 'Shop Vac Bag' — actual: 'The Reusable Shop Vac Bag That Saves Crews Thousands.'
Homepage subhead contains '12-20 gallon', 'HEPA', 'shop vac dust bag', and '$99'
No forbidden patent tokens (US Patented / Patented / patent holder) on / or /ads (in rendered DOM)
Homepage <title> exact match: 'Reusable Shop Vac Bags & Filter Bags, 12–20 Gal | Muk Buddy'
JSON-LD Product schema has exactly 1 Product entity with mpn=MUKBUD1420, price=99.00, currency=USD, NO hasMerchantReturnPolicy, additionalProperty includes 'Design: Patent-Pending 2-Chamber'
/ads trust strip pills present (Patent-Pending design / Made in the USA / Built for daily contractor use / Secure checkout)
/ads GuaranteeBar has 4 tiles (Made in the USA / Patent-Pending Design / Built for Daily Use / Secure Checkout) with NO '30-day return' or 'Free shipping' anywhere on page
/about h1 = 'Built by a contractor. Tested by 800 jobs.' and contains 'Jason Brouk' and 'patent pending'

All 6 blog posts have correct YouTube iframe with expected video ID (jK-Ocvafh24, qR-Ow0woAmw, HwfeHKGOzQM, KkaqjZcYN0s, Ks0oA7Pnogc, 8wKa1mR66gk) using https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/{id}?rel=0
Author bio text exact match on the 6 video posts: 'Founder of Floor Lord Industries and inventor of Muk Buddy. 28 years doing floors — and still building the tools contractors wish already existed.'

Homepage lead form (name/email/phone/crew_size) submits → POST /api/leads → 201 → redirects to /thank-you?lead_id=… AND fires window.gtag('event','conversion',{send_to:'AW-1058129782/0FzTCNaD7AgQ9o7H-AM', value:99, currency:'USD', transaction_id:<uuid>}) AND window.fbq('track','Lead',{value:99, currency:'USD', content_name:'Main Site Lead Form'})
/ads lead form with machine_count=1 fires gtag value=99 and fbq value=99 (1 × $99)
/ads lead form with machine_count=7 fires gtag value=693 and fbq value=693 num_items=7 (7 × $99 — dynamic multiplication confirmed)
No customer-assets.emergentagent.com asset references on /; all images load (0 broken)
/llms.txt returns 200 from CDN with first heading '# Muk Buddy' and contains '12–20 gallon', 'Made in the United States' / 'Manufactured in: United States', and 'Patent Pending' / 'patent-pending'
action_items: FIX /ads meta robots → noindex, follow (verify PageHead/Helmet runs and that the production server serves the prerendered build/ads/index.html which already has the correct tag)
FIX /about copy: replace 'filed a US patent application' with a phrasing that uses ONLY 'patent pending' (e.g., 'has a patent-pending 2-chamber design')

FIX /blog/disposable-vs-reusable-shop-vac-bags/: restore author=Jason Brouk in the .md frontmatter so the byline says 'By Jason Brouk' and the author bio block renders. The post was likely overwritten during the GitHub manual-commit sync. Re-run /app/scripts/build-blog.js after fixing.

FIX deployment: the Express server is serving the static public/index.html SPA shell for all routes (28KB) instead of the per-route prerendered HTML in /app/frontend/build/{route}/index.html (49KB+). This is invisible to humans but devastating for SEO (the whole point of this sprint). Inspect the production server config — it should fall through to build/{route}/index.html before the catch-all build/index.html.
Verify /llms.txt is rendered (currently 200 via curl/CDN; some clients with non-browser User-Agent get 403 from Cloudflare — likely fine)
critical_code_review_comments: Lead form value calc on /ads: select options use cardinal midpoints (1=1, 3=2-4 midpoint, 7=5-10 midpoint, 12=10+) — that's a reasonable proxy for $99-multiplication, but be aware the dollar value reported to Google Ads is approximate (a crew with 4 machines bills 297 instead of 396). Fine for ad bidding but worth documenting.

Spec said homepage crew_size enum should be '1', '2-5', '6-15', '16-50', '50+' but actual options are '1 crew', '2-4 crews', '5-9 crews', '10+ crews' (string values, not numeric). This is just a documentation mismatch; the form still submits + tracks correctly.

Hero h1 reads 'The Reusable Shop Vac Bag That Saves Crews Thousands.' — there's a trailing period followed by a giant decorative 'MUK BUDDY' div. Fine, but worth noting the h1 text-content ends with '.'

Homepage form fires fbq('track','PageView') TWICE (Pixel registers two PageViews) — likely the base pixel snippet + a route-change PageView. Mild but worth investigating to avoid double-counting in Meta.
Express x-powered-by header is exposed (security smell, low). Disable with app.disable('x-powered-by').

success_rate.frontend: ~85% (17/20 spec items pass in rendered DOM; 3 HIGH bugs + 1 deployment/SEO issue)

seed_data_creation: Created 3 TEST_ leads in MongoDB via /api/leads during form testing: test_ads_lead@example.com (machine_count=1), test_ads7@example.com (machine_count=7), test_home_lead2@example.com (crew_size=2-4 crews). Filter by name LIKE 'TEST%' to clean up.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Preview URL https://two-chamber-test.preview.emergentagent.com. Server is Express and serves public/index.html shell for all routes (28KB) instead of the prerendered build/{route}/index.html (49KB+). Therefore curl-based static HTML assertions fail; use Playwright DOM checks instead. Homepage lead form: actual crew_size option values are '1 crew','2-4 crews','5-9 crews','10+ crews' (NOT '1','2-5','6-15','16-50','50+'). /ads form: machine_count option values are '1','3','7','12' (midpoints of range labels '1 machine','2-4 machines','5-10 machines','10+ machines'); crew_count option values are '1','2-4','5-9','10+'. Both forms route to /thank-you?lead_id=<uuid> after successful POST /api/leads. gtag + fbq are monkey-patchable BEFORE submit to capture call args. To verify a fix for /ads noindex, fetch with curl AND with Playwright — both should show 'noindex, follow' in <meta name=robots>.
rca of the issue:
Three independent bugs surfaced by this verification:
1) /ads not noindex: PageHead/Helmet for /ads sets noindex but the served public/index.html shell carries a site-wide 'index, follow, max-image-preview:large' default. Helmet rewrites it at runtime IF /ads is hydrated client-side, but our test reads the static meta after hydration — and even after hydration the meta still showed 'index, follow' (helmet may not be applied or is overridden). Local build/ads/index.html DOES carry the correct noindex when prerender runs. Solution: ensure prerendered HTML is actually being served per route AND verify AdsLanding's <PageHead robots='noindex, follow'/> is wired.
2) /about 'US patent application': literal copy in /app/frontend/src/pages/About.jsx contains 'Floor Lord Industries filed a US patent application on the 2-chamber design.' This violates the strict 'no US patent' rule on /about. Manual copy edit needed.
3) Blog post disposable-vs-reusable-shop-vac-bags author byline shows 'By Muk Buddy' and the founder bio block is missing. The user's manual GitHub commit e450508 modified .md files; specifically this post's frontmatter likely lost author: 'Jason Brouk' or the bio rendering condition. Inspect /app/content/blog/disposable-vs-reusable-shop-vac-bags.md frontmatter vs the other 6 posts; restore author and any bio toggle, then rerun /app/scripts/build-blog.js.
